rulings.Case Title: DEBBIE RAMIREZ et al VS. CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Intent To Move For New Trial After Granting Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 30, 2008 LINE 1. PLAINTIFFs DEBBIE RAMIREZ, ROBERT BURKE AND BRENDAN BURKE'S Notice Of Intent To Move For New Trial After Granting Summary Judgment: The motion for a new trial is denied. Plaintiffs have not effectively demonstrated why the evidence they seek to introduce was not produced before. It is undeniable from plaintiffs' opposition that plaintiffs understood that the propensity for crime in the parking lot location was an issue to be addressed and plaintiffs presented evidence on this issue. The declarations submitted by the police officers in the motion for a new trial explain why they would not have left the youth in the parking lot. It is not the Court's conclusion that it was a prudent decision by the CHP officers to leave the young men in the parking lot. In deciding this case, the question is one of the forseeability of harm that resulted from the officers' actions. It is the Court's conclusion that it is simply not forseeable that a man with a gun would shot the young men in this parking lot at 3:00 a.m., whether the assailant be a stranger or, as in this case, a man with a gun who had a motive to kill one or both of the young men. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: WESTERN GRAVEL COMPANY VS. RICHARD KISER et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SONOMA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Change Of Venue And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 30, 2008 LINE 17. DEFENDANTS RICHARD KISER AND SHANNON KISER'S Motion For Change Of Venue And For Sanctions. THE MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS RESIDE IN SONOMA COUNTY. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THIS IS A MIXED ACTION CASE ENTITLING DEFENDANTS TO HAVE THE ACTION VENUED IN SONOMA COUNTY. THE VENUE PROVISION RELIED ON BY PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENFORCEABLE BOTH AS A MATTER OF LAW AND AS A MATTER OF FACT, FOR NO SUCH AGREEMENT WAS REACHED. GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S UNREASONABLE REFUSAL TO TRANSFER THE CASE, DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO REASONABLE COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $8,747.50. =(302/PJM/VC)
